about.title: About
about.subtitle: Our Songs for Peace
about.desc1: We first sang in front of Gureombi Rock in Gangjeong Village, Jeju, where the rough winds blow. We realized that peace is not a grand slogan, but begins the moment we listen to each other's voices.
about.desc2: Gangjeong Peace Music Camp is a solidarity and festival of musicians dreaming of a world without war. Beyond borders and languages, we speak of peace with music, the most beautiful weapon.
about.desc3: When we sing, dance, and embrace each other together, war will stop and the waves of peace will spread further.

card1.title: Sustainable Peace
card1.desc: The first harmony began in the summer of 2023. Our festival will continue not as a one-time event, but as a persistent and beautiful ritual of peace guarding the summer of Gangjeong every year.

card2.title: Solidarity Without Borders
card2.desc: We remember conflict zones from the Gaza Strip in Palestine to Ukraine. Gangjeong Peace Music Camp goes beyond a simple concert; it is a powerful platform of solidarity standing with those suffering and yearning for peace.

gallery.page_desc: Moments of us singing for peace. Explore vivid photos from the Gangjeong Peace Music Camp.
gallery.section_subtitle: A record of the wind and songs of Gangjeong
gallery.image_alt_fallback: Gangjeong Peace Music Camp Gallery Image

intro.heading: Photographs from where Gangjeong village, music, and peace meet
intro.p1: Gangjeong sits on the southern coast of Jeju, looking out to Beomseom islet beneath Mount Halla. Decades after the 4·3 massacre, a new layer of history began in 2007 when residents started resisting a naval base. This gallery preserves the atmosphere of concerts held on that very shoreline.
intro.p2: The images span the 2023, 2025, and the ongoing 2026 camps. Alongside musicians on stage you will find haenyeo (Jeju women divers), Gureombi-rock activists, children in the crowd, and Peace Center volunteers — all hinting at the community beyond the festival itself.
intro.p3: Tapping a card opens the full-resolution image in a lightbox. Non-commercial use for peace and music movement contexts is welcome with attribution.

press.page_desc: Our story singing for peace. Check out the press coverage and articles about the project 'To You in a Distant Place Whose Name I Don't Know'.

intro.heading: How the world reported a small village that sang for peace
intro.p1: The Gangjeong Peace and Music Camp is an annual independent festival in Gangjeong, a small village on Jeju Island that has resisted naval base construction since 2007. This archive gathers reporting from Korean dailies, independent music webzines, and international peace outlets that have covered the camp since its first edition in 2023.
intro.p2: Korean-language pieces tend to dig into Jeju's post-4·3 history and village-level storytelling, while Japanese and English coverage frames the camp alongside other East Asian base struggles in Okinawa, Guam, and the Philippines. Each card opens the original article on the publisher's own site.
intro.p3: Coverage spikes around each festival window and the links below preserve lineups, crowdfunding milestones, and on-site photography for future reference. Use the filter chips to narrow results by year or event.

videos.page_desc: Collection of performance videos from Gangjeong Peace Music Camp. Meet the performance videos of musicians singing for peace.

intro.heading: Live performances from a village that refused to stay silent
intro.p1: Most clips gathered here are field recordings from Gangjeong village and small spaces across Korea. Unlike commercial festivals, the cameras are one or two, the sound is raw PA, and the audience often dances while facing a naval base just beyond the stage.
intro.p2: Korean indie, folk, punk, and traditional musicians deliver anti-war, anti-base, and environmental messages in their own language. You'll hear Ritesh Maharjan weaving Nepali-Korean roots music, the protest blues of touring songwriter Kim Dong-san, and live DJ sets by Josuganman — the spectrum is deliberately wide.
intro.p3: Use the filter chips to narrow to the 2023, 2025, or 2026 camps, or to the 2024 studio album. Selecting any card opens a dedicated page on this site — with performance date, venue, and performer context — rather than jumping straight to YouTube.

camp.not_found_desc: The requested camp information could not be found.
camp.description_2026: The 3rd Gangjeong Peace Music Camp will be held from June 5 (Fri) to June 7 (Sun), 2026, at Gangjeong Sports Park in Seogwipo, Jeju. Marking the 73rd anniversary of the Korean War armistice, the camp expands for the first time into a 3-day, 2-night format with 51 acts. As militarization and the threat of war intensify on the Korean Peninsula, the song of peace that began in Gangjeong Village grows into a stronger and wider solidarity. Under the slogan "Let's sing, let's dance, end the war!", musicians from diverse genres deliver messages of peace and anti-war through music. Tickets are on sale at tumblbug.com/gpmc3.
camp.seo_title_2026: 3rd Gangjeong Peace Music Camp 2026 · Jun 5–7 · Jeju · 51 Acts
camp.seo_description_2026: The 3rd Gangjeong Peace and Music Camp — June 5–7, 2026 at Gangjeong Village, Jeju Island. 51 Korean indie acts across three days of peace music at Gangjeong Sports Park. Tickets ₩30,000 via Tumblbug (tumblbug.com/gpmc3).
camp.slogan_2026: Let's sing, let's dance, end the war!
camp.seo_musician_suffix: Performing at the 3rd Gangjeong Peace & Music Camp, June 5-7, 2026 at Gangjeong Sports Park, Jeju.
camp.cta_final_body: Help the song of peace that began in Gangjeong reach further. Support us on Tumblbug.

camp-2023.title: 1st Gangjeong Peace Music Camp
camp-2023.short_description: The first camp singing for peace in Gangjeong Village
camp-2023.description: The first Peace Music Camp held in Gangjeong Village on June 10, 2023. Under the slogan "End the war! Play, dance, and sing!", we shared solidarity and friendship within the Gangjeong peace movement.
camp-2023.location: Gangjeong Sports Park (669 Ieodo-ro, Seogwipo-si, Jeju)
camp-2023.slogan: End the war! Play, dance, and sing!

camp-2025.title: 2nd Gangjeong Peace Music Camp
camp-2025.short_description: On the 72nd anniversary of the armistice, raise the voices for peace
camp-2025.description: The second Peace Music Camp held across Gangjeong Village, Jeju, on June 14, 2025. Marking the 72nd anniversary of the armistice, it delivered a message of peace against the intensifying militarization of the Korean Peninsula.
camp-2025.location: Gangjeong Sports Park (2661, Gangjeong-dong, Seogwipo-si, Jeju) and around Gangjeong Village
camp-2025.slogan: Let's sing, let's dance, end the war!

Marking the 73rd anniversary of the Korean War armistice, the camp expands for the first time into a 3-day, 2-night format with 51 acts. As militarization and the threat of war intensify on the Korean Peninsula, the song of peace that began in Gangjeong Village grows into a stronger and wider solidarity. Under the slogan "Let's sing, let's dance, end the war!", musicians from diverse genres deliver messages of peace and anti-war through music. Tickets are on sale at tumblbug.com/gpmc3.

structured_data.project_name: To You in a Distant Place Whose Name I Don't Know
structured_data.description: A project of musicians delivering a message of peace through music in a world where war and violence are prevalent
structured_data.music_group_desc: A musical project singing for peace
structured_data.playlist_desc: Collection of music from the project 'To You in a Distant Place Whose Name I Don't Know'
structured_data.gallery_desc: Photo gallery of the project 'To You in a Distant Place Whose Name I Don't Know'
structured_data.howto_name: How to Attend Gangjeong Peace Music Camp
structured_data.howto_step1_text: Visit peaceandmusic.net to check the camp dates and lineup. The 3rd camp in 2026 runs from June 5 (Fri) to June 7 (Sun).
structured_data.howto_step2_text: Take a bus or taxi from Jeju Airport to Gangjeong Village in Seogwipo. Car rental is also available.
structured_data.howto_step3_text: Buy a ticket through Tumblbug crowdfunding (tumblbug.com/gpmc3); your purchase directly supports the camp.
structured_data.howto_step4_text: Arrive at Gangjeong Sports Park and enter with the Tumblbug ticket you purchased.
structured_data.howto_step5_text: Watch performances by musicians of diverse genres and share in the spirit of peace solidarity.

camp_faq_2026.q1: When is the 3rd Gangjeong Peace and Music Camp?
camp_faq_2026.a1: June 5–7, 2026, a three-day peace music festival in Gangjeong Village with 51 acts.
camp_faq_2026.a2: Gangjeong Sports Park in Gangjeong Village, Seogwipo, Jeju Island, South Korea. Accessible by bus or taxi from Jeju Airport.
camp_faq_2026.a3: Yes — admission is ticketed. Tickets are ₩30,000 (KRW) via Tumblbug crowdfunding (tumblbug.com/gpmc3); proceeds fund the festival and peace solidarity work.
camp_faq_2026.q4: How can I support the camp?
camp_faq_2026.a4: Buying a ticket on Tumblbug (tumblbug.com/gpmc3) directly supports the camp. You can also help by buying merchandise on site.
camp_faq_2026.a5: 51 Korean indie artists across genres including Yun Sunae, Jeong Jinseok, Choi Sangdon, Kim Dongsan & Blue Neighbors, Lim Jeongdeuk, Taehyeon, HANASH, and TAGI.
camp_faq_2026.q6: Where can I see the full timetable?
camp_faq_2026.a6: The complete 3-day schedule is at peaceandmusic.net/camps/2026, sorted chronologically by performance time.
